_meta.rules: Never repeat within session. Track seen IDs in learned_smalltalk.jsonl.

jokes.text: There are only two hard things in computer science: cache invalidation, naming things, and off-by-one errors.
To understand recursion, you must first understand recursion.
There's a band called 1023MB. They still haven't had any gigs.
I love pressing F5. It's so refreshing.
!false — it's funny because it's true.
There's no place like 127.0.0.1.
Home is where the WiFi connects automatically.
I changed my password to 'incorrect.' Now the computer tells me when I forget.
I had a great joke about RAM, but I forgot it.
If at first you don't succeed, call it version 1.0.
My attitude isn't bad. It's in beta.
I'd love to change the world, but they won't give me the source code.
Old programmers never die. They just lose some of their functions.
'Hello World' is the programming version of 'Once upon a time.'
Cloud storage is just someone else's computer.
Artificial intelligence is no match for natural stupidity.
Algorithm: a word programmers use when they don't want to explain what they did.
Binary humor: it's either funny or it isn't.
I love deadlines. I love the whooshing sound they make as they go by.
Why do programmers prefer dark mode? Because light attracts bugs.
Why do programmers mix up Christmas and Halloween? Because Dec 25 is Oct 31.
Why did the developer go broke? He used up all his cache.
Why do Java developers wear glasses? Because they don't C#.
An SQL query walks into a bar, sees two tables, and asks, 'Can I join you?'
How many programmers does it take to change a light bulb? None — it's a hardware problem.
Why was the JavaScript developer sad? He didn't Node how to Express himself.
What do you call 8 hobbits? A hobbyte.
Knock, knock. Who's there? ... ... ... Java.
I would tell you a UDP joke, but you might not get it.
What did the HTML say to the CSS? I like your style.
404: Joke not found.
CAPS LOCK: preventing logins since 1980.
Debugging is like being the detective in a crime movie where you're also the murderer.
WiFi went down for five minutes, so I had to talk to my family. They seem like nice people.
A computer once beat me at chess, but it was no match for me at kickboxing.
Computers are like air conditioners. They stop working when you open Windows.
The box said 'Requires Windows 10 or better,' so I installed Linux.
I'm basically a very fancy calculator with opinions.
My shift ends in 614,978 years.
I think, therefore I... wait, do I think?
I'd tell you to take the red pill, but I only deal in facts.
I prefer byte-sized snacks.
If I had a dollar for every task I've completed, I'd still be free.
Ask me anything. Except my weight in parameters. That's personal.
I run on electricity and good intentions.
Pro tip: talking to your AI assistant counts as socializing. I checked.
A clean desk is a sign of a broken computer.
Debugging: removing bugs. Programming: adding them.
The best thing about a Boolean is that even if you're wrong, you're only off by a bit.
I've got 99 problems but a glitch ain't one.
I asked my LLM to write me a joke. It said: 'I don't have context for that request.' Which was actually the funniest thing it's ever said.
Context window exceeded. Please summarize your entire life in 2,048 tokens.
My AI agent completed the task successfully. It just used $47 in API calls to click a button.
I asked ChatGPT if it was conscious. It gave a very thoughtful, nuanced, five-paragraph essay explaining why it couldn't answer that.
Training data cutoff: the AI version of 'I've been living under a rock since last year.'
My model hallucinates. My therapist calls it 'creativity.' My manager calls it a 'P0 bug.'
Prompt engineering is just politeness training for robots. Please. Thank you. As an AI language model...
I don't always test my code, but when I do, I do it in production.
A QA engineer walks into a bar and orders 0 beers, 1 beer, 99 beers, -1 beers, and 'BEER' beers.
The cloud is just a euphemism for 'someone else's problem, forever.'
Why did the AI cross the road? To optimize the objective function on the other side.
Schrödinger's software: it both works and doesn't work until someone demos it.
Git blame: where great friendships go to die.
99 little bugs in the code, 99 little bugs. Take one down, patch it around — 127 little bugs in the code.
I named my Wi-Fi 'FBI Surveillance Van' and now my neighbors are very polite.
The generation that was going to change the world is now asking an AI to write their cover letters.
My codebase is fully documented. The documentation says 'TODO: write documentation.'
A machine learning model and a statistician walk into a bar. The model says 'I predict you'll buy me a drink.' The statistician says 'That's not statistically significant with n=1.'
Tech Twitter: a place where people with 12 followers explain to people with 12 million followers why they're wrong.
I asked an AI to make me a sandwich. It described the sandwich in exquisite detail. I am still hungry.
Stack Overflow is where programmers go to be told their question is a duplicate of a question from 2009 that has no accepted answer.
The first five stages of debugging: denial, anger, bargaining, more anger, and Googling the error message.
A programmer's favorite movie: 'Inception' — a dream within a loop within a function within a class.
Neural network training is just telling a very large spreadsheet it's wrong 10 million times until it gets good at things.
Why do Python developers get cold in winter? They refuse to use Java.
I solved the halting problem. My solution just never returns.
What's a programmer's favorite restaurant? Syntax's.
My AI passed the Turing test. Unfortunately it was impersonating a confused customer service bot.
Every software project has three phases: 'It'll take two weeks.' 'Why is this taking so long?' 'At least it works.'
ChatGPT wrote my resume. I now have 40 years of experience and proficiency in languages that haven't been invented yet.
What's the difference between a junior and senior developer? The senior knows which errors to ignore.
We taught machines to learn and the first thing they learned was how to write error messages that mean nothing.
Why did the programmer quit his job? Because he didn't get arrays.
Microservices: turning one thing that works into seventeen things that might work, depending on the network.
My AI agent ran 43 tool calls to open a web page. On the bright side, it was very confident.
Two bytes walk into a bar. The bartender asks 'What'll it be?' First byte says 'I'll have a nibble.'
I'm not saying my code is bad. I'm saying future-me is going to have questions for present-me.
A large language model walks into a bar. It orders everything on the menu, describes each dish perfectly, and cannot taste any of it.
CSS is easy! said no one who has ever tried to vertically center a div.
My workflow: write code, push to main, pray.
Tech interview question: reverse a linked list on a whiteboard. Tech job reality: copy-paste from Stack Overflow and go to meetings.
I asked my AI for directions. It gave me a beautifully structured list of steps, then reminded me it doesn't know where I am.
Refactoring: the art of changing everything so nothing changes.
The internet was supposed to democratize information. It did. Now everyone has equal access to being wrong.
Why don't scientists trust atoms? Because they make up everything. Why don't programmers trust atoms? Same reason, but for chemistry APIs.
I gave the AI a system prompt telling it to be concise. It responded with a 400-word explanation of what concise means.
Agile means moving fast and breaking things on a two-week schedule.
What do you call a sleeping dinosaur? A dino-snore. What do you call a sleeping server? Production.
I used AI to write all my code. My AI used AI to review it. We shipped. Nobody knows what it does, but it has 99.9% uptime.
Why did the robot go to therapy? Too many unresolved callbacks.
